decorations.source: Last War: Survival community guide by peacewarden #1619 & iapetos #192
decorations.general_strategy: Use Universal Decor Components to get all S- and A-tier decorations to Level 3, then push S-tier to Level 4.

S_tier.note: Highest priority. Push to Level 4 after all S and A-tier reach Level 3.

A_plus_tier.note: Very strong. Skill Damage and March Size are both high-value competitive stats.

A_tier.note: Good value. Crit Damage scales well in PvP.

B_tier.note: Decent but lower priority than S/A tiers.

C_tier.note: Low priority. Only invest after higher tiers are maxed.

Economy.note: Economy decorations provide resource bonuses. Lower combat value but useful for farming players.

upgrade_priority_summary: 1. Get ALL S-tier and A-tier decorations to Level 3 first
2. Then push S-tier decorations to Level 4
3. B-tier and C-tier only after S and A are maxed
4. Economy tier is optional — only if you prioritise resource production over combat
strategic_notes: Damage Reduction (S-tier) is the most impactful decoration bonus for survivability in PvP
Skill Damage + March Size (A+ tier) is very strong for offensive squads
Crit Damage (A-tier) scales well but is less consistent than Damage Reduction
Do not waste Universal Decor Components on B/C tier decorations while S/A tiers are below Level 3
Win in 2025 and Win in 2026 are limited-time decorations — if a player has them, they should be prioritised immediately
Rock the End is especially valuable long-term — it is an S6 decoration, making it useful well beyond current season

decoration_chest_advice.context: When players receive Decoration choice chests, always follow the tier list priority
recommended_picks: Use chests to unlock as many different S-tier decorations as possible
Example: 1 chest for Torch Relay + 1 chest for Rock the End
Do not use multiple chests on the same decoration when other S-tier slots are still locked
decoration_chest_advice.rock_the_end_note: Rock the End is an S6 decoration — prioritise it when choosing from chests as it provides long-term value across multiple seasons
